view.planModeTitle: Plan mode: AI proposes but does not execute write actions
view.actModeTitle: Act mode: AI executes actions with approval
view.yoloOnTitle: YOLO mode ON — all tool calls auto-approved
view.yoloOffTitle: Click to enable YOLO mode (auto-approve all actions)
view.emptySubtitle: Messages are stored locally and encrypted at rest
view.emptyFolderContext: AI can read and create entities in "{{name}}"
view.selectThread: Select a thread to view the conversation
view.extensionRequired: Browser extension or team server connection required
view.deleteThreadMessage: This chat thread will be moved to trash. Are you sure?
view.deleteFolderMessage: This folder and its organization will be removed. Threads inside will be moved to the top level.
view.rewindMessage: This will delete {{count}} message(s) after this point. This cannot be undone. Use Branch instead to preserve the full history.
view.restoreMessage: This will undo the AI's changes — created entities will be deleted and modified entities will be reverted to their previous state. This cannot be undone.
view.switchedToPlan: Switched to Plan mode — AI will propose without executing
view.switchedToAct: Switched to Act mode — AI will execute with approval
view.switchedToActMessage: Switched to **Act mode**. Send "execute the plan" to run the proposed actions with approval gating, or continue the conversation.
view.errorCreateThread: Failed to create chat thread. Try refreshing the page.
view.errorNoLocalEndpoint: No Local LLM endpoint configured. Add it in Settings → AI/LLM.
view.errorNoApiKey: No {{provider}} API key configured. Add an API key or local LLM endpoint in Settings → AI/LLM.
view.noApiKeysConfigured: No API keys configured — add one in Settings → AI/LLM
view.onboardingStep1Desc: Go to Settings > CaddyAI / LLM and add an API key for at least one provider or configure a local LLM endpoint.
view.onboardingStep2Desc: CaddyAI requires the ThreatCaddy browser extension to proxy API requests.
view.onboardingStep3Desc: In the extension popup, enable "Allow CaddyAI" for AI provider access and "Allow URL fetching" for the /fetch tool.

input.noApiKeys: No API keys configured — add one in Settings → AI/LLM
input.extensionRequiredDesc: The ThreatCaddy browser extension is required for CaddyAI to make API calls. Install it from the
input.placeholderReady: Ask CaddyAI anything... (/ for commands, @ to mention, paste images)
input.placeholderDisabled: Configure a local LLM or install the extension

message.undoTitle: Undo: restore entities to before this action
